mnemonics: Whitespace Is Invisible, But Not Ignored - It structures code like paragraphs structure an essay.
keyPoints: Whitespace (spaces, tabs, newlines) separates keywords and identifiers.
The compiler largely ignores extra whitespace for execution, but it is crucial for human readability.
Whitespace does not separate operators or separate parts of a single keyword/identifier (e.g., 'int number' is two tokens, 'intnumber' is one).
commonMistakes: Inconsistent indentation, making code blocks hard to read.
Adding spaces in multi-word identifiers (e.g., 'my variable' instead of 'myVariable').
Forgetting that string literals preserve all whitespace within the quotes.
